mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and raise equity discount rates, directly compressing valuation multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above the 0.2 percentage point threshold signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in triggered instances show positive direction in > 55% of cases, or out-of-sample predictive accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate a synchronized collapse in household demand and construction activity, which transmits negative earnings shocks to the broader market and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Joint negative signals fail to predict negative 20-day returns with > 55% accuracy, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which boosts corporate profit margins and generates positive short-term equity returns during recovery phases.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO revisions are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC drops below 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly expand aggregate income and consumption capacity, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns during positive payroll revisions show negative direction in > 60% of instances, or predictive IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation accelerates beyond the 3.0 percent annual threshold, central banks tighten policy and real discount rates rise, compressing equity multiples and generating negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns after inflation threshold breaches are positive in > 58% of cases, or threshold model fails to outperform linear baseline by > 1% accuracy.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating real GDP growth and rising unemployment creates a negative feedback loop that reduces business investment and consumer confidence, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression, or 20-day returns during dual-signal periods are not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate alongside stable or falling inflation signals a proactive easing cycle that lowers financing costs without eroding real returns, generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns during rate cuts with stable inflation are negative in > 55% of instances, or joint signal predictive accuracy falls below 50%.
